vocab.meaning: bắt nguồn từ (nguồn cảm hứng hoặc bối cảnh)
vocab.example: While modern art stems from a desire for individual expression, traditional art was often rooted in religious patronage.

vocab.meaning: phản kháng hoặc đi ngược lại (các chuẩn mực cũ)
vocab.example: Modern art movements frequently push back against the rigid academic standards that defined the classical era.

vocab.meaning: phát triển chi tiết hơn (ý tưởng hoặc hình ảnh)
vocab.example: Old masters would often flesh out their paintings with intricate details that took years to complete, unlike some minimalist modern works.

vocab.meaning: lược bỏ, gạt bỏ (những thứ rườm rà)
vocab.example: Contemporary artists often strip away unnecessary ornamentation to focus on the raw, conceptual core of the piece.

vocab.meaning: nhìn lại (một thời kỳ trong quá khứ)
vocab.example: When we look back on classical art, we see a heavy emphasis on realistic proportions and technical mastery.

vocab.meaning: trở nên khác biệt/tách biệt hoàn toàn
vocab.example: Digital art stands apart from traditional oil painting because of its infinite capacity for manipulation and reproduction.

vocab.meaning: khai thác (một cảm xúc, xu hướng hoặc công nghệ)
vocab.example: Modern installations often tap into the viewer's subconscious through immersive technology and soundscapes.

vocab.meaning: mở rộng sang (một lĩnh vực hoặc phong cách mới)
vocab.example: In the 20th century, art began to branch out into multimedia formats, moving beyond just canvas and stone.

vocab.meaning: hòa hợp với (không gian hoặc bối cảnh)
vocab.example: Traditional sculptures were designed to blend in with classical architecture, whereas modern pieces often intentionally clash with their surroundings.

vocab.meaning: truyền tải (thông điệp hoặc ý tưởng)
vocab.example: The abstract nature of modern art can make it harder for the artist to get their message across to a general audience.
